title: 10 best herbal tinctures for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for swollen gums are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs that are used to treat inflammation and pain in the gums. They are a popular natural remedy for gum problems because they offer a safer and gentler alternative to over-the-counter medications. Some examples of herbal tinctures that help with swollen gums include goldenseal, echinacea, myrrh, and calendula. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling, fight infection, and soothe pain. By using herbal tinctures, people can alleviate the discomfort and embarrassment of swollen gums, improve their oral health, and enhance their quality of life. For instance, a person who suffers from chronic gum inflammation due to gum disease or dentures can use herbal tinctures to manage their symptoms and prevent further complications, such as tooth loss or bone damage. Herbal tinctures can also help people who have sensitive gums due to orthodontic treatment or injury to speed up the healing process and avoid painful irritations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The herb, Achillea millefolium, is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins that work together to soothe irritated gum tissue, reduce pain, and promote healing. Yarrow tincture can also increase blood flow to the gums, which can help to speed up the healing process. Its astringent properties can help to tighten gum tissue, reducing the risk of further infection and inflammation. Overall, yarrow tincture is a natural and effective remedy for swollen gums, promoting oral health and hygiene.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce swelling and irritation in gums.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tincture, owing to the presence of volatile oils like azulene, aids in inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum infection and swelling.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, caused by tannins, helps tighten and heal damaged gum tissues, reducing swelling and promoting oral health.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tincture, due to the presence of alkaloids, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.

Wound healing: Yarrow tincture's wound healing property, resulting from its rich antioxidant profile, supports the healing of inflamed and damaged gum tissues, reducing swelling and fostering oral health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in gums.
Leaves: The leaves of yarrow have antimicrobial and astringent qualities, making them useful for treating swollen gums.
Stems: Yarrow stems also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in gums.
Roots: Yarrow roots contain high levels of antioxidants and have been shown to have a positive effect on oral health, making them a good choice for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the early morning for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using scissors or a knife to release essential oils.
Combine 20-30% of the chopped plant material with 70-80% of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties that soothe inflammation and irritation in the mouth. The active compounds in marshmallow root, such as mucilage, form a protective layer over the gums, reducing pain and promoting healing. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat various oral health issues, including swollen gums, mouth ulcers, and gingivitis. Marshmallow tincture's soothing and calming effects on the mucous membranes make it an effective natural alternative for alleviating discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for swollen gums as it helps to alleviate pain and redness. This property is attributed to the mucilage content of marshmallow root.

Demulcent: The soothing and healing effect of marshmallow tincture on mucous membranes helps to protect and relieve irritated gums. This is due to the high mucilage content, which forms a protective layer over the affected area.

Antibacterial: Marshmallow tincture can help inhibit the growth of bacteria, contributing to reduced infection and inflammation in swollen gums. The antibacterial properties come from the tannins and flavonoids present in marshmallow root.

Analgesic: Marshmallow tincture can provide pain relief for swollen gums, making it easier to eat and maintain oral hygiene. This is due to the anti-inflammatory and anesthetic effects of the mucilage and flavonoids.

Astringent: Marshmallow tincture can help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation. This astringent property is attributed to the tannins found in marshmallow root.
remedy_parts: Roots: The marshmallow roots contain mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums.
Leaves: Marshmallow leaves also contain mucilage, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation in swollen gums.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating swollen gums.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage and has a soothing effect on swollen gums.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean fresh althaea officinalis roots and leaves.
Dry the herbs using air circulation or shade drying methods.
Chop and grind the dried herbal material into a coarse texture.
Steep the chopped herb in high-proof grain alcohol for 2 weeks.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or coffee filters.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The herbs used in kinnikinnick tincture, such as willow bark and blackberry root, have been traditionally used to treat gum diseases and oral infections. These herbs contain compounds that can help to decrease swelling, fight bacteria, and promote healing in the gums. Additionally, kinnikinnick tincture can help to improve blood circulation in the gums, which can further aid in the healing process. By using kinnikinnick tincture regularly, individuals can experience relief from swollen gums and maintain good oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kinnikinnick tincture contains compounds such as tannins and essential oils that help reduce inflammation, which is crucial for alleviating swollen gums.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of kinnikinnick tincture, due to the presence of compounds like berberine, help control bacterial growth and infections in the gums.
Astringent: Kinnikinnick tincture contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to tighten and reduce swelling in swollen gums.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of kinnikinnick tincture, attributed to compounds like lignans, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by swollen gums.
Immune-boosting: Kinnikinnick tincture can help strengthen the immune system, thanks to compounds like polyphenols, which is important for managing and preventing swollen gums caused by infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling in gums.
Bark: Rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can soothe irritated gums.
Leaves: Have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections in swollen gums.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that can reduce inflammation and provide relief from swollen gums.
Buds: Rich in essential oils that have analgesic properties, providing relief from gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems, wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the harvested material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to achieve a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion of the plant's properties.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in an amber glass bottle, tightly capped, and label it with date prepared and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as alkaloids and flavonoids, work together to soothe irritated gum tissue, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote healthy gum tissue growth. Additionally, wild indigo tincture can help boost the immune system, which can speed up the healing process and prevent further infection. Its natural astringent properties can also help tighten gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum disease and other oral health issues. Overall, wild indigo tincture is a safe and effective natural remedy for treating swollen gums and maintaining optimal oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Wild indigo tincture can reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, due to the presence of alkaloids like indigotine and indirubin.
Antimicrobial property: The tincture can help control gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, thanks to the presence of isoflavonoids and other phenolic compounds.
Analgesic property: Wild indigo tincture can alleviate gum pain, as it contains compounds like indican and baptisin that help numb the affected area.

Immunostimulant property: The tincture can enhance the body's immune response, allowing it to better fight infections causing swollen gums, due to the presence of polysaccharides and other immune-stimulating compounds.
Astringent property: Wild indigo tincture can help tighten gum tissues and reduce gum inflammation by causing contraction of body tissues, owing to the presence of tannins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild indigo contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for reducing swelling and pain in gums.
Leaves: The leaves of wild indigo are rich in antimicrobial compounds, which can help to reduce infection and inflammation in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers of wild indigo contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe swollen gums and promote healing.
Seeds: The seeds of wild indigo contain saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help to reduce swelling in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried wild indigo leaves and stems in late summer or early fall for best potency.
Chop 100 grams of the plant material into small pieces and combine with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the final tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can stimulate the flow of saliva, which helps to keep the mouth clean and prevent infection. Its astringent properties can also help to tighten and tone gum tissue, reducing the risk of further inflammation. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a natural and effective remedy for swollen gums, providing relief and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of volatile oils and alkaloids, inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing by stimulating tissue regeneration, which is important for repairing damaged gum tissue, due to its high content of tannins and saponins.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of pot marigold tincture, caused by the presence of triterpenes and flavonoids, help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
Antioxidant: Its potent antioxidant properties, derived from carotenoids and flavonoids, protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and support overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for reducing swelling in gums.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat gum inflammation, thanks to their soothing and calming effects.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of the pot marigold plant also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning, selecting only bright yellow petals and discarding any brown or wilted material.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently blowing air through them and then spreading them out on a paper towel for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all flowers are fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily for ten seconds to facilitate extraction, allowing the vodka to extract the bioactive compounds.

After the extraction period, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting tincture in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain. The active compounds in chamomile, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, have been shown to help decrease inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth. Additionally, chamomile has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate discomfort and provide relief to irritated gums. Overall, german chamomile tincture can be an effective natural remedy for swollen gums, promoting oral health and hygiene.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce swelling in gums by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals, thanks to its active constituent, chamazulene.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help control infections causing gum inflammation, as it contains active components like alpha-bisabolol and matricin.

Analgesic: German chamomile tincture alleviates pain from swollen gums due to its analgesic effects, attributed mainly to the active compound matricin, which gets converted to anti-inflammatory agent matricarin.
Wound healing: Its wound-healing properties promote gum tissue repair and regeneration, facilitated by the presence of flavonoids and other active constituents.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tincture has potent antioxidant effects, which help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and support overall oral health, due to the presence of various antioxidant compounds, including terpenoids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for treating swollen gums.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a rich source of essential oils, including bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dry dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla, ensuring quality and potency.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces and mix with a carrier oil such as glycerin or vegetable oil at 1:1 ratio.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, allowing it to steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place, using within 6 months to a year for optimal potency and quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. This herbal remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including gum disease. The active compounds in eastern purple coneflower, such as echinacea, work to stimulate the immune system and promote healing. When applied directly to the affected area, the tincture can help to alleviate symptoms of swollen gums, providing relief and promoting overall oral health. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to over-the-counter medications and a valuable addition to any oral hygiene routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as chamazulene and eudesmanolides, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in gums.
Antimicrobial: The tincture has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids, which can help control bacterial infections causing gum inflammation.
Analgesic: Eastern purple coneflower tincture contains analgesic compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its rich content of antioxidant flavonoids, which support gum tissue repair and recovery.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tincture has immune-boosting effects due to its polysaccharide content, helping strengthen the body's defenses against oral infections that can lead to gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help reduce swelling in gums.
Leaves: Packed with soothing properties, the leaves provide relief to irritated gums.
Buds: Containing high levels of antioxidants, the buds aid in healing swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: With potent antibacterial properties, the rhyzomes help eliminate the cause of gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Cleanly chop 30-40 grams of herb material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped herb with 60% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, covering herb by at least 2 inches.
Store jar in a cool dark place and steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and storing tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tincture is made from the leaves of the teaberry plant, which is rich in tannins and essential oils that have astringent properties, helping to tighten and strengthen gum tissue. Additionally, teaberry has natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. Regular use of teaberry tincture can help to soothe and relieve swollen gums, promoting overall oral health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Teaberry tincture's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling in gums by limiting the production of compounds that cause inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like tannins and volatile oils.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial property of teaberry tincture helps control gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for infections, due to the presence of compounds like ursolic acid and gallic acid.

Analgesic: Teaberry tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate pain from swollen gums by numbing the affected area, which is attributed to its active constituents, such as methyl salicylate and eugenol.

Astringent: The astringent property of teaberry tincture helps reduce gum swelling by causing contraction of tissues, providing relief from inflammation, and promoting healing, due to its high tannin content.

Antioxidant: Teaberry tincture's antioxidant property supports gum health by neutralizing harmful free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting tissue repair, thanks to its rich supply of antioxidants, like flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins and essential oils, known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in gums.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and astringent compounds, it can help soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in swollen gums.
Buds: Packed with active compounds, buds can provide a potent extract for relieving pain and reducing swelling in gums.
Roots: Contain a high concentration of medicinal compounds, making them ideal for creating a tincture that can reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems at peak growth stage, typically in summer months.
Wash the harvested material gently with pure water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop the clean plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Mix 1 part teaberry plant material with 2 parts high-proof vodka or ethanol in a glass jar, filling to the top.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally, before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and soothe irritation. The tannins present in witch hazel act as an astringent, helping to tighten and strengthen gum tissues, which can reduce swelling. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help to fight off any bacterial infection that may be causing the gum inflammation, promoting overall oral health. Regular use of witch hazel tincture can provide relief from painful and swollen gums, leading to improved oral hygiene and fresher breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and soothe irritated gum tissues, reducing swelling and promoting healing, due to the high tannin content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel contains antioxidants that protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can worsen gum inflammation, attributed mainly to the presence of gallic acid and procyanidins.
Analgesic: Witch hazel has pain-relieving properties that alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums, as it contains several analgesic compounds, such as safrole and eugenol.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of witch hazel tincture help control bacterial growth in the mouth, preventing infections that can exacerbate gum swelling, owing to the presence of various antimicrobial constituents, such as gallic acid, tannins, and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains tannins that have astringent properties, making it effective at reducing inflammation and swelling in gums.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing swollen gums.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain volatile oils and tannins that have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making them helpful in reducing swelling in gums.

Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant contain volatile oils and tannins that have astringent and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in the early morning, using clean scissors and gloves to avoid contamination.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 1-2 weeks, monitoring humidity and temperature.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle, then combine with high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5 (plant material to solvent).
Allow the mixture to steep in a dark glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily, before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Store the resulting tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from light and heat, for up to 2 years while monitoring its color, odor, and consistency regularly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains several active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria. When applied directly to the gums, the tincture can help to soothe irritation, reduce swelling, and promote healing. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, making it a useful tool in maintaining oral health. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to traditional mouthwashes and medications, providing a holistic approach to dental care.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation in swollen gums, thanks to the presence of active compounds like hypericin and hyperforin.
Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections, with the help of active constituents like hyperforin and flavonoids.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture alleviates pain associated with swollen gums, due to the pain-relieving properties of hypericin and other phenolic compounds.
Astringent: It helps tighten and heal damaged gum tissue, as a result of tannins and other astringent compounds found in the tincture.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tincture neutralizes free radicals, promoting gum tissue health and reducing inflammation, with the help of antioxidant-rich compounds like hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for reducing swelling and irritation in gums.
Leaves: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping alleviate pain and swelling in gums.
Buds: Packed with active compounds, buds can provide a potent extract for treating gum inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly within three days.
Combine one part dried herb with two parts vodka or high-proof spirits in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily during preparation.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Label and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid, using within two years after preparation date.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal tinctures is by using a tincture of echinacea, goldenseal, or myrrh. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce swelling and fight infection. Dilute the tincture in water and use it as a mouthwash, swishing it around your mouth for several minutes before spitting it out. Repeat this process several times a day, especially after meals and before bedtime. In addition to using herbal tinctures, maintain good oral hygiene by brushing and flossing regularly. If swelling persists, consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal tinctures include gingivitis and periodontitis. Gingivitis is inflammation of the gums, while periodontitis is a severe form of gum disease that can lead to tooth loss. Herbal tinctures such as goldenseal, myrrh, and echinacea can be used to treat these conditions due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immune-boosting properties. Goldenseal and myrrh have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight bacteria in the mouth, while echinacea can boost the immune system and promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal tinctures to treat any medical condition.
